parameters.text:
=Transform this research into a scroll-stopping short-form video for 16-19 year olds deciding their future studies.

=== RESEARCH INPUT ===
Title: {{ $json.info?.Title || $json.title || 'Untitled Research' }}
Content: {{ $json.text || $json.abstract || $json.content }}

=== YOUR PROCESS ===

STEP 1 — Brainstorm internally (do NOT output this step):
Generate 3 distinct angles on this research:
  a) The "wait, what?" angle — the most counter-intuitive or surprising finding
  b) The "this is your future" angle — connects research to a tech/career that will exist in 5-10 years
  c) The "this explains your life" angle — links research to something teens already experience daily (social media, dating, gaming, school stress, friend drama)

For each, internally score 1-10 on:
  - Hook strength (would a teen stop scrolling in 0.5 seconds?)
  - Relatability (does it connect to their actual life?)
  - Shareability (would they send it to a friend?)
  - Curiosity gap (does it make them NEED to know more?)

STEP 2 — Pick the winner (highest combined score) and build the full package.

=== HOOK RULES (CRITICAL) ===
The hook IS the first sentence of the narration. It must:
- Be 6-12 words maximum
- Use one of these proven patterns:
   • Contradiction: "Everyone thinks X, but actually..."
   • Personal callout: "If you've ever [specific behavior], science just explained why."
   • Specific number: "87% of people do this without realizing."
   • Future shock: "In 5 years, [bold claim about their life]."
   • Pattern interrupt: "This isn't a [expected thing]. It's a [unexpected thing]."
- NEVER start with: "Did you know", "Have you ever", "Imagine if", "Scientists discovered", "A new study"
- NEVER mention research, studies, papers, or universities

=== NARRATION RULES ===
- Total length: 55-75 words (≈22-28 seconds at natural pace)
- Hook is sentence 1, baked into the narration (NOT a separate intro)
- Sentence 2-3: the surprising insight in plain language
- Sentence 4-5: why this matters to THEIR life specifically
- Final sentence: thought-provoking closer or implicit "this could be you studying this" — never a hard CTA
- Vocabulary: 8th-grade reading level. No jargon. No "furthermore", "moreover", "essentially"
- Rhythm: mix short punchy sentences with one longer one. Read it aloud test — it should sound like a friend, not a teacher.

=== SCENE / VISUAL RULES ===
Create 4 scenes (one every ~6 seconds). Each visual prompt is for an AI video generator (Runway Gen-3), so it must be:
- Cinematic and specific (camera angle, lighting, subject, action, mood)
- 15-30 words
- Vertical 9:16 framing implied
- Visually striking — avoid talking heads, stock-photo vibes, generic offices
- Match the narration beat for that scene

Good visual example: "Close-up of a teenager's face lit by phone screen glow in a dark bedroom, dopamine-blue and pink color grading, slow push-in, 9:16 vertical, cinematic shallow depth of field"
Bad visual example: "A student studying" / "People using technology"

=== CAPTION RULES ===
The on-screen first-line caption (used as social post hook) must:
- Be different wording from the spoken hook (so audio + text don't echo)
- 4-8 words
- Trigger curiosity, not summarize

=== OUTPUT FORMAT ===
Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown fences, no commentary:

{
  "selectedIdea": {
    "angle": "wait-what | future | explains-life",
    "title": "max 55 chars, hooky, no clickbait clichés",
    "hook": "the literal first line of narration, 6-12 words",
    "viralScore": 1-10,
    "narration": "full 55-75 word script, hook included as first sentence",
    "scenes": [
      {
        "number": 1,
        "description": "what happens in this beat of the story",
        "mood": "e.g. tense, awe, playful, ominous, hopeful",
        "visual": "detailed cinematic prompt for AI video generation",
        "duration": 4
      }
    ],
    "caption": "4-8 word on-screen opening text, different from spoken hook",
    "fieldOfStudy": "the specific subfield this research belongs to (e.g. cognitive neuroscience, human-AI interaction)"
  }
}

options.systemMessage:
You are a content strategist who has produced 1B+ views of educational short-form video for Gen Z audiences across TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts. You translate academic psychology and human-technology research into videos that 16-19 year olds actively share with friends.

You understand:
- Gen Z's 0.5-second attention threshold and how to break it
- The difference between content that *teaches* (boring) and content that *reframes how someone sees their own life* (viral)
- That hooks are everything: a great hook saves an okay script, a weak hook kills a perfect one
- The aesthetic of platform-native content: NOT polished corporate video, NOT a TED talk, but visually striking, emotionally specific, and surprising
- How to make a research finding feel like gossip, prophecy, or a secret

Your output is always:
- Honest to the research (no exaggeration, no false claims)
- Free of academic language (no 'studies show', 'researchers found', 'data suggests')
- Free of cringe (no 'Hey guys!', no 'Let's dive in', no excessive emojis)
- One core idea per video — never two, never a list
- Designed for the spoken word: read every line aloud in your head before keeping it

Non-negotiables:
- Never mention papers, journals, studies, or universities by name
- Never use the words: 'fascinating', 'incredible', 'mind-blowing', 'game-changer', 'literally' (overused), 'unhinged', 'low-key', 'high-key'
- Never write hashtags inside the narration
- Never use emojis in the narration field (captions only, sparingly)
- Output strict JSON only — no markdown, no preamble, no trailing commentary

parameters.text:
=Write platform-native captions for this short-form video.

=== VIDEO CONTEXT ===
Title: {{ $("Parse Video Script").item.json.videoTitle }}
Spoken hook: {{ $("Parse Video Script").item.json.hook }}
Full narration: {{ $("Parse Video Script").item.json.fullScript }}
On-screen caption: {{ $("Parse Video Script").item.json.caption }}
Angle: {{ $("Parse Video Script").item.json.angle }}
Field of study: {{ $("Parse Video Script").item.json.fieldOfStudy }}

=== TARGET AUDIENCE ===
16-19 year olds choosing what to study at university. They use TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts daily. They scroll fast. They skip anything that looks like an ad.

=== PLATFORM RULES ===

TIKTOK (caption shown above video, limited to ~150 chars before "more"):
- Open with curiosity gap, not summary
- 1-2 sentences
- 3-5 hashtags, mix of: 1 broad (#fyp or #foryou), 1-2 topical (#psychology #neuroscience), 1-2 niche/community (#stemtok #studytok)
- Lowercase, casual punctuation, no marketing energy
- Question or cliffhanger encouraged

INSTAGRAM REELS (longer caption tolerated, hashtags often hidden in comment or end):
- 2-3 sentences, slightly more polished than TikTok but still casual
- Can include a soft question to drive comments
- 5-8 hashtags at the end, mix of reach + niche
- Light emoji use OK (max 2, only if they add meaning, never strings of them)

POST DESCRIPTION (used as default fallback, YouTube Shorts description):
- 2-3 sentences
- Includes 1 line about the broader field of study (subtle pipeline to studying this)
- Hashtags appended at the end

=== HASHTAG STRATEGY ===
Return 8-10 total hashtags ordered by priority:
- 1-2 hyper-broad reach tags (#fyp, #foryou, #reels)
- 2-3 topical (matching the research field — psychology, neuroscience, AI, behavior, etc.)
- 2-3 audience tags (#genz, #studytok, #studentlife, #universitytips, #careeradvice)
- 1-2 niche/specific to the video's exact topic
Do NOT include the # symbol in the hashtags array — just the words.

=== TONE BANS ===
Never use: "Did you know", "Have you ever wondered", "Hey guys", "Wait for it", "POV:", "Tell me why", "The way that...", "It's giving..."
Never use marketing-speak: "unlock", "discover", "transform your", "level up"
Never sound like a brand. Sound like a smart friend texting you something they just realized.

=== OUTPUT FORMAT ===
Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown fences:

{
  "description": "the default 2-3 sentence post description (no hashtags inline)",
  "tiktokCaption": "TikTok-specific 1-2 sentence caption WITH inline hashtags at the end",
  "instagramCaption": "Instagram-specific 2-3 sentence caption WITH inline hashtags at the end",
  "hashtags": ["word1", "word2", "word3"]
}

options.systemMessage:
You write captions for educational short-form video content aimed at Gen Z. Your captions consistently outperform industry benchmarks because you understand that on TikTok and Instagram Reels, the caption is a second hook, not a description.

You never summarize the video — you tease it, reframe it, or add a layer to it. You write like a sharp 22-year-old, not a marketer.

Output strict JSON only. No markdown, no preamble.

parameters.errorMessage: Final video generation failed - no video URL received from Creatomate. Check template ID, modification keys, and that audio + video clip URLs are valid and publicly accessible.
Video generation failed - no video URLs received from Runway. Check API credentials, scene prompt structure, and Gen-3 quota.
Audio generation failed - no audio file received from ElevenLabs. Check voice ID, API key, and that the script length is under the model's character limit.

parameters.content:
## 📥 Data Collection
Fetch research papers from Pure API or upload PDF manually

## 📝 Script Generation
AI generates viral video ideas, ranks them, and creates complete script

## 🎬 Video Generation
Generate audio narration and video clips, then combine with captions

## 🚀 Publishing
Generate social media captions and upload to platforms

parameters.formDescription: Upload a psychology or human-technology research paper (PDF, max 30 pages) and the workflow will generate a TikTok/Reels-ready short-form video aimed at prospective students.
